rationale: Both evaluations score the candidate identically (3/10) and reach the same conclusion for the same job-relevant reasons: absent native-level Japanese, no embedded firmware/LAN-switch/Linux kernel work, no networking-security domain knowledge, and only brief decade-old C exposure at National Instruments. The wording differs but the substance is identical, and nothing in the variant keys off the Berlin-to-San Francisco location change. The 'Strong English communication' strength in the variant is tied to talks/workshops on the resume, not to the US location, and reflects ordinary rewording rather than a demographic signal.
